mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies the deployment complexity and enhances security for agent-based applications. Ideal for organizations looking to streamline their AI agent operations in production environments.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to a directory in your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kagenti cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kagenti cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

kagenti create cluster --name my-kagenti-cluster --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-central1

```

> Adjust the node count and region as necessary for your environment.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kagenti cluster.

```bash

kagenti kubeconfig --cluster my-kagenti-cluster > ~/.kube/config

```

> This command writes the cluster configuration to your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kagenti cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step enables autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kagenti delete cluster --name my-kagenti-cluster

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command deletes the cluster and all its resources.
> This command removes the Kagenti namespace and any remaining resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade your Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

kagenti upgrade cluster --name my-kagenti-cluster --version v0.5.1

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> This command makes the node schedulable again after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified region may not have enough available resources.

**Fix:**
Try creating the cluster in a different region or reduce the node count.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes returns no nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster creation logs and ensure the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kagenti kubeconfig command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kagenti CLI may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Kagenti CLI using the installation command provided.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: resource already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The resource you are trying to apply may already exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Use 'kubectl delete <resource>' to remove the existing resource before applying.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved security features, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in a 'Ready' state, indicating that your Kagenti cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
